title: salix × kotuliana
intro: Salix × kotuliana, also known as Kotula's willow, is a hybrid plant that combines the characteristics of Salix alba and Salix fragilis. The bark and leaves of this plant contain salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Salix × kotuliana are said to provide relief from pain, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of rheumatism and arthritis, thus offering several health benefits to individuals who consume it. Herbal preparations of Salix × kotuliana include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically to soothe sore muscles and joints.
